skill.name: executing-marketing-campaigns
skill.description: Plans marketing campaigns, creates channel-specific content, and defines measurement workflows for email, social, paid, content, and launch programs.
skill.summary: Build campaign plans, messaging, templates, tracking links, and reporting frameworks for marketing teams.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many hits, but most are false positives from Markdown code fences, directory trees, placeholder URLs, and words such as description or keywords. The confirmed risk is a bundled Python utility that reads a user-supplied CSV path and can write a user-supplied output CSV path, so publication should include a filesystem access warning.

medium_findings.title: User-Controlled Local File Read and Write
medium_findings.description: The utility script accepts a CSV input path and optional CSV output path from command-line arguments. This supports legitimate batch UTM generation, but users should run it only on intended local files because it can read arbitrary CSV paths and overwrite the chosen output path.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly opens paths supplied through --file and --output. The behavior is explicit and useful, but it creates a real local filesystem risk if run with untrusted paths.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Execution Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The external command findings are shell examples inside documentation and directory reference blocks. They do not execute unless a user intentionally runs the documented marketing utility commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are fenced examples or quick-reference documentation. No dynamic shell execution API is present in the Python helper.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The URL findings are example marketing destinations or URL parsing inputs. No HTTP client call or outbound network request was found in the executable script.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script imports urllib.parse utilities and validates URL strings, but it does not import requests, urllib.request, or another network client.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance findings are caused by ordinary marketing words, placeholders, and documentation terms. No cryptographic function, credential harvesting, prompt injection, or reconnaissance workflow was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Representative hits are terms such as description, designed, keywords, and local config variable names. Targeted review found no crypto API use or exfiltration intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: File Writes to Caller-Supplied Output Path
dangerous_patterns.description: Batch mode writes generated tracking data to the path supplied with --output. This is useful but should be disclosed as local filesystem access.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file write is direct and intentional. It is not malicious by itself, but it can overwrite a caller-selected CSV path.

content.value_statement: Marketing teams need consistent plans, messages, and measurement before launch. This skill provides campaign frameworks, channel templates, UTM utilities, and reporting guidance for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
actual_capabilities: Guides campaign planning from objectives through audience, messaging, channel mix, timeline, and KPIs.
Provides templates for emails, landing pages, paid ads, content briefs, campaign briefs, and case studies.
Offers platform guidance for LinkedIn, X, Facebook, Instagram, YouTube, email, paid search, and content marketing.
Defines measurement approaches, funnel metrics, attribution models, dashboard ideas, and reporting cadences.
Includes a Python utility for UTM parameter generation, tracking URL validation, batch CSV processing, and CSV export.
Supports brand consistency through voice, tone, visual, accessibility, and terminology guidelines.
limitations: It does not connect directly to ad platforms, email tools, analytics products, or CRM systems.
Its benchmarks and platform guidance should be verified against current channel policies and company data.
The utility script only generates and validates tracking URLs; it does not publish or monitor campaigns.
Company-specific brand rules, budgets, audience research, and compliance requirements must be added by the user.

use_cases.title: Launch a Product Campaign
use_cases.description: Create the campaign brief, launch message, channel plan, email sequence, tracking URLs, and success metrics.

use_cases.title: Build a Lead Generation Program
use_cases.description: Plan a lead magnet, landing page, nurture emails, social promotion, paid ads, and reporting framework.

use_cases.title: Standardize Campaign Operations
use_cases.description: Give teams reusable templates, naming conventions, UTM rules, review checklists, and reporting formats.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Campaign Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a campaign plan for [product or offer]. Include objective, audience, message, channels, timeline, and KPIs.

prompt_templates.title: Draft Channel Content
prompt_templates.prompt: Create email, LinkedIn, paid search, and landing page copy for [campaign]. Use our audience [audience] and value proposition [value proposition].

prompt_templates.title: Set Up Measurement
prompt_templates.prompt: Define a measurement plan for [campaign]. Include funnel metrics, UTM naming, dashboard sections, reporting cadence, and optimization decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Audit and Optimize a Campaign
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this campaign plan and performance summary: [details]. Identify weak assumptions, missed segments, channel risks, test ideas, and next actions.

output_examples.input: Plan a launch campaign for a new B2B analytics feature.
output: Campaign objective, audience segments, positioning statement, and message pillars.
Channel plan for email, LinkedIn, paid search, landing page, and sales enablement.
Launch timeline, owner checklist, UTM naming plan, and KPI dashboard outline.

output_examples.input: Create a webinar lead generation plan for finance leaders.
output: Audience hypothesis, webinar promise, registration page sections, email invite sequence, and social posts.
Lead scoring signals, nurture follow-up structure, and conversion metrics.
Diagnosis of subject line, offer, segmentation, send timing, deliverability, and call to action.
A/B test plan with variants, success metrics, sample size notes, and next-step decisions.

best_practices: Provide current audience data, budget, timeline, offer, brand rules, and channel constraints before asking for campaign assets.
Validate platform guidance, legal requirements, and performance benchmarks before launch.
Use the UTM utility in a controlled working directory and review output paths before writing CSV files.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the generated templates as final copy without brand, legal, and factual review.
Do not use generic audience segments when campaign performance depends on precise buyer context.
Do not run the utility script against untrusted CSV files or write outputs to sensitive paths.

faq.question: What types of campaigns does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports product launches, lead generation, retention, awareness, newsletters, paid ads, content programs, and social campaigns.

faq.question: Does it publish campaigns to marketing platforms?
faq.answer: No. It creates plans, copy, templates, tracking URLs, and reporting guidance, but it does not publish to external platforms.

faq.question: Can it generate UTM tracking links?
faq.answer: Yes. The included Python script can generate UTM parameters, build tracking URLs, validate URLs, and process campaign CSV files.

faq.question: Does it include brand guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes voice, tone, visual, accessibility, terminology, and campaign creative guidance that users can customize.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a warning. The main concern is local file read and write behavior in the optional Python utility.

faq.question: What should users customize first?
faq.answer: Users should add their audience segments, brand standards, approved claims, channel rules, analytics tools, and performance targets.
